content.text: Plain objects and arrays cover most data needs, but JavaScript has two specialized collection types —
— that solve specific problems more efficiently.
stores unique values with no duplicates.
stores key-value pairs where the key can be any type (not just strings). In React, these appear in scenarios like managing selected IDs, caching computed lookups, and deduplicating data.
is a collection of unique values. Adding a value that already exists is silently ignored. Sets maintain insertion order.
The Most Useful Set Pattern: Deduplication
is a collection of key-value pairs where
any type can be a key
— not just strings like in plain objects. It also maintains insertion order and has convenient methods for common operations.
Mostly preserved (not guaranteed for all keys)
For most React state (JSON data from APIs), plain objects are preferred. Use
when you need non-string keys, exact insertion-order iteration, or frequent add/delete operations on large datasets.
O(1) Lookup with Map (Normalizing API Data)
stores unique values — duplicates are silently ignored. Use it to deduplicate arrays (
) or track a collection of unique IDs (like selected items).
stores key-value pairs with any key type. Use it when you need non-string keys, guaranteed iteration order, or a clean lookup structure without prototype pollution.
are valid state values — but you must create new instances when updating (
are O(1) operations — much faster than
serializes to JSON directly — convert to arrays or objects when storing to APIs or localStorage.
